behavior_summary: ARMRIGHT::use marks the referenced object with an info key (0x0207), prevents further processing of that object (process-exclude), and spawns a BODYPART process (id 0x0270) for the object — likely to attach or animate the right arm on U8P_ARMRIGHT::use.

ARMRIGHT::hit handles a hit/damage event: it sets a different info key (0x020B) and process-excludes the object, frees/clears a resource identified by 0x0202 on that object, queries how many processes of type 0x020A exist for the object, and if that query indicates presence it spawns a BODYPART process (id 0x0431) — likely a hit reaction or visual effect.

The script uses info flags (0x0207 / 0x020B) as state markers, process-exclude to prevent concurrent processing, and spawn/free/getNumProcesses engine primitives to manage per-object body-part behaviors and cleanup.
notable_control_flow: On 'U8P_ARMRIGHT::use' event: set info 0x0207 on the target -> set process-exclude -> spawn BODYPART::0270(target, 0). The spawn return value is popped/stored then ignored.

On 'hit' event: set info 0x020B on the target -> set process-exclude -> call FREE::2A56(0x0202, target) to remove a resource -> call Kernel::getNumProcesses(target, 0x020A) -> if result indicates existing processes (inferred: >0) then spawn BODYPART::0431(target, 0).
No global flags are written or read by these functions in this script excerpt — behavior is confined to per-object state and processes.
open_questions: Exact semantic meaning of info keys 0x0207 and 0x020B (they appear to be per-object state markers related to 'used' and 'hit' but exact game-state implications are unknown).
Precise interpretation of ProcessType 0x020A and resource id 0x0202 (what kinds of processes/resources they represent at gameplay level).
Whether BP+06 is always the object's own PID ('self') or sometimes another target object (inferred as the object this body-part belongs to but not proven).
The exact condition tested on Kernel::getNumProcesses (inferred to be >0) that gates spawning BODYPART::0431.

main_function.name: U8P_ARMRIGHT::use
main_function.purpose: Respond to a 'U8P_ARMRIGHT::use' interaction on the right-arm body-part by marking the target object (info 0x0207), excluding it from further automatic processing, and spawning a BODYPART::0270 process to create the visual/behavioral effect (attachment/animation) for that object.
entry_conditions: Event 1 (U8P_ARMRIGHT::use) — invoked when the actor/player 'uses' or activates the right-arm body part.

function_map.name: U8P_ARMRIGHT::use
function_map.role: Handle 'U8P_ARMRIGHT::use' on right-arm body part: set per-object state and spawn body-part process (BODYPART::0270).

function_map.name: U8P_ARMRIGHT::hit
function_map.role: Handle 'hit' on right-arm: set hit state, free/clear an attached resource (0x0202), query process counts (0x020A), and conditionally spawn a hit-response body-part process (BODYPART::0431).

external_calls.purpose: Create/start a body-part process (id 0x0270) for the target object to produce attach/animation/effect on U8P_ARMRIGHT::use.
Free or clear a resource/attachment identified by id 0x0202 on the target object as part of hit cleanup.
Query the number of processes of ProcessType 0x020A for the object to decide whether to spawn a hit-response process.
Conditionally create/start a body-part process (id 0x0431) for the object — likely a visual/damage reaction spawned when processes of type 0x020A exist.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: ARMRIGHT::use marks the referenced object with an info key (0x0207), prevents further processing of that object (process-exclude), and spawns a BODYPART process (id 0x0270) for the object — likely to attach or animate the right arm on U8P_ARMRIGHT::use.
